chapters.title: The Subjunctive Mood
chapters.description: Master the subjonctif to express doubt, emotion, and necessity.

lessons.prompt: Which expression triggers the subjunctive?
lessons.explanation: 'Il faut que' (it is necessary that) always takes subjunctive. Expressions of certainty use indicative.

lessons.prompt: Je veux qu'il ___ la vérité. (I want him to tell the truth.)
lessons.explanation: 'Vouloir que' triggers subjunctive. Subjunctive of 'dire' for il = dise.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Although she is tired, she continues working'
lessons.correctAnswer: Bien qu'elle soit fatiguée, elle continue à travailler
lessons.hint: 'Bien que' (although) always takes subjunctive. 'Soit' = subjunctive of être.

lessons.prompt: What is the subjunctive of 'avoir' for 'nous'?
lessons.explanation: Irregular subjunctive of avoir: que j'aie, tu aies, il ait, nous ayons, vous ayez, ils aient.

lessons.prompt: Il est important que vous ___ à l'heure. (It's important that you arrive on time.)
lessons.explanation: 'Il est important que' takes subjunctive. Subjunctive of arriver for vous = arriviez.

learningMaterial.title: The Subjunctive Mood
learningMaterial.intro: Master the subjonctif to express doubt, emotion, and necessity.

concepts.explanation: 'Il faut que' (it is necessary that) always takes subjunctive. Expressions of certainty use indicative.
'Vouloir que' triggers subjunctive. Subjunctive of 'dire' for il = dise.

concepts.term: Bien qu'elle soit fatiguée, elle continue à travailler
concepts.explanation: 'Bien que' (although) always takes subjunctive. 'Soit' = subjunctive of être.
Irregular subjunctive of avoir: que j'aie, tu aies, il ait, nous ayons, vous ayez, ils aient.
'Il est important que' takes subjunctive. Subjunctive of arriver for vous = arriviez.

chapters.title: Idiomatic Expressions
chapters.description: Master everyday French idioms that natives use constantly.

lessons.prompt: What does 'avoir le cafard' mean?
lessons.explanation: Literally 'to have the cockroach' — a common idiom meaning to feel blue or depressed.

lessons.prompt: Il pleut des ___. (It's raining cats and dogs.)
lessons.explanation: 'Il pleut des cordes' literally means 'it's raining ropes' — the equivalent of 'raining cats and dogs'.

lessons.prompt: Translate the idiom: 'To cost an arm and a leg'
lessons.correctAnswer: Coûter les yeux de la tête
lessons.hint: French version literally means 'to cost the eyes from the head'.

lessons.prompt: What does 'casser les pieds à quelqu'un' mean?
lessons.explanation: Literally 'to break someone's feet' — means to be really annoying.

lessons.prompt: Ne vends pas la peau de l'ours avant de l'___. (Don't count your chickens before they hatch.)
lessons.explanation: Literally: don't sell the bear's skin before killing it. Equivalent of 'don't count your chickens'.

learningMaterial.title: Idiomatic Expressions
learningMaterial.intro: Master everyday French idioms that natives use constantly.

concepts.explanation: Literally 'to have the cockroach' — a common idiom meaning to feel blue or depressed.
'Il pleut des cordes' literally means 'it's raining ropes' — the equivalent of 'raining cats and dogs'.

concepts.term: Coûter les yeux de la tête
concepts.explanation: French version literally means 'to cost the eyes from the head'.
Literally 'to break someone's feet' — means to be really annoying.
Literally: don't sell the bear's skin before killing it. Equivalent of 'don't count your chickens'.

chapters.title: Formal Writing & Nuance
chapters.description: Write formal letters, emails, and nuanced arguments in French.

lessons.prompt: Which is the correct formal letter closing?
lessons.explanation: This highly formal closing is used in official correspondence. 'Cordialement' is semi-formal.

lessons.prompt: Suite à notre entretien, je me ___ de vous confirmer... (Following our interview, I am writing to confirm...)
lessons.explanation: 'Je me permets de' = I take the liberty of / I am writing to. A very formal construction.

lessons.prompt: Translate formally: 'I would like to draw your attention to'
lessons.correctAnswer: Je souhaiterais attirer votre attention sur
lessons.hint: 'Souhaiterais' is the conditional of 'souhaiter'. More formal than 'je veux'.

lessons.prompt: What does 'nonobstant' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Nonobstant' is a formal/legal term meaning notwithstanding or nevertheless. Common in formal writing.

lessons.prompt: Dans l'attente de votre ___, je vous adresse mes cordiales salutations. (Awaiting your reply...)
lessons.explanation: 'Dans l'attente de votre réponse' = awaiting your reply. Standard formal email closing phrase.

learningMaterial.title: Formal Writing & Nuance
learningMaterial.intro: Write formal letters, emails, and nuanced arguments in French.

concepts.explanation: This highly formal closing is used in official correspondence. 'Cordialement' is semi-formal.
'Je me permets de' = I take the liberty of / I am writing to. A very formal construction.

concepts.term: Je souhaiterais attirer votre attention sur
concepts.explanation: 'Souhaiterais' is the conditional of 'souhaiter'. More formal than 'je veux'.
'Nonobstant' is a formal/legal term meaning notwithstanding or nevertheless. Common in formal writing.
'Dans l'attente de votre réponse' = awaiting your reply. Standard formal email closing phrase.

chapters.title: Conditional & Hypotheticals
chapters.description: Discuss hypothetical situations and unrealised conditions.

lessons.prompt: Complete: Si j'avais de l'argent, je ___ un château.
lessons.explanation: Si + imparfait → conditionnel présent. This is the hypothetical/unreal condition pattern.

lessons.prompt: Si elle avait étudié, elle ___ réussi. (If she had studied, she would have passed.)
lessons.explanation: Si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé. 'Aurait réussi' = would have passed.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'If I were president, I would change this law'
lessons.correctAnswer: Si j'étais président, je changerais cette loi
lessons.hint: Imparfait after 'si', then conditionnel présent in the main clause.

lessons.prompt: What tense follows 'si' in a real/likely condition?
lessons.explanation: Si + présent → futur simple. Ex: 'S'il pleut, je prendrai un taxi.' (If it rains, I will take a taxi.)

lessons.prompt: Au cas ___ il viendrait, prévenez-moi. (In case he comes, let me know.)
lessons.explanation: 'Au cas où' = in case. It always takes the conditionnel in the following clause.

learningMaterial.title: Conditional & Hypotheticals

concepts.explanation: Imparfait after 'si', then conditionnel présent in the main clause.
Si + présent → futur simple. Ex: 'S'il pleut, je prendrai un taxi.' (If it rains, I will take a taxi.)
'Au cas où' = in case. It always takes the conditionnel in the following clause.
